articles.title: I went to the Pentagon to watch Pete Hegseth scold war reporters
articles.description: It is day 13 of America's surprise war with Iran - by sheer coincidence, it's Friday the 13th - and I am delirious. I haven't had a coffee since I woke up at 5AM, because I'm not allowed to bring outside beverages into the Pentagon (the security screening cutoff was at 7AM for the 8AM), […]
articles.author: Tina Nguyen

articles.title: The Beats Studio Pro are nearly $200 off ahead of Amazon’s big spring sale
articles.description: Earlier this week, Apple unveiled the AirPods Max 2. The forthcoming over-ear headphones build on their predecessor with an H2 chip that powers a range of new features; however, they still don’t offer the sort of cross-OS compatibility you’ll get with a modern pair of Beats headphones — including the Beats Studio Pro, which are […]
articles.author: Brandon Widder

articles.title: Remedy’s live-service shooter Firebreak is getting its final major update
articles.description: Remedy is winding down its team shooter FBC: Firebreak with a big update that launches today. But while the game won't be getting any new content going forward, the studio intends to keep it alive for the foreseeable future. It's yet another example of a live-service game struggling amidst a volatile market. The new update […]
articles.author: Andrew Webster

articles.title: Spotify adds 'Exclusive Mode' audiophile feature for Windows PCs
articles.description: Starting Tuesday, Spotify users will have access to "Exclusive Mode," a new feature in the streaming service's Windows app that can help maximize audio quality by giving the Spotify app full control over your device's audio processing. According to Spotify, "your computer may alter audio before it reaches your DAC by resampling it, mixing other […]
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: Ikea tried to build a smart home for everyone — here's why it's not working yet
articles.description: Ikea's new Matter-over-Thread products were supposed to prove that the smart home could be cheap, accessible, and reliable. The highly anticipated line - which includes sensors, remotes, smart plugs, air-quality monitors, and smart bulbs - has most everything you need to build a smart home, with prices starting at $6. It's an exciting idea, but […]
articles.author: Jennifer Pattison Tuohy

articles.title: Nvidia's DLSS 5 is like motion smoothing for video games, but worse
articles.description: Yesterday Nvidia revealed its latest upscaling tech, called DLSS 5, which it described as "the company's most significant breakthrough in computer graphics since the debut of real-time ray tracing in 2018." Sounds good, until you actually see it. According to Nvidia, the tech "infuses pixels with photoreal lighting and materials," but all anyone seemed to […]
articles.author: Andrew Webster

articles.title: Antigravity’s 360-degree drone is 20 percent off ahead of its next feature drop
articles.description: Insta360’s Antigravity A1 is one of the most interesting drones on the market thanks to its included Vision Goggles, which provide a 360-degree first-person view through the drone’s cameras (a screen on the outside of its lenses shows what the pilot is seeing). Its cameras can shoot 8K video at 30 frames per second, or […]
articles.author: Cameron Faulkner

articles.title: The messiah rises in Dune: Part Three’s new trailer
articles.description: First comes love, then comes marriage, then comes a portentous set of twin babies in the new trailer for Dune: Part Three. Warner Bros. has debuted its first full trailer for Denis Villeneuve's third Dune film. Thankfully, it doesn't give everything about the film away, but it does put a spotlight on a few of […]
articles.author: Charles Pulliam-Moore

articles.title: Now everyone in the US is getting Google's personalized Gemini AI
articles.description: Google announced on Tuesday that all users in the US will now have access to its Personal Intelligence feature, which lets you connect various Google apps to provide context for Gemini's responses and suggestions. Access was previously limited to Google AI Pro and AI Ultra subscribers. Now, free-tier users in the US can also use […]
articles.author: Stevie Bonifield

articles.title: Intel announces Core Ultra 200HX Plus CPUs for high-end gaming laptops
articles.description: Intel has a pair of new flagship CPUs coming to a variety of pricey gaming laptops: the Core Ultra 9 290 HX Plus and Core Ultra 7 270HX Plus. The Arrow Lake Refresh chips sport 24 cores / 24 threads and 20 cores / 20 threads, respectively. Like Intel's recently announced desktop CPUs, the new […]
articles.author: Antonio G. Di Benedetto

articles.title: The Live Nation trial restarts with a 'velvet hammer'
articles.description: After a chaotic week following the Justice Department's mid-trial settlement with Live Nation-Ticketmaster, the antitrust trial picked back up surprisingly smoothly on Monday - this time, with dozens of states leading the case. This isn't the outcome the states originally wanted. Out of concerns about being able to effectively take over the case and fear […]
articles.author: Lauren Feiner
